editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

##Install/Activate Correct Seurat after Downgrading for Integration
```{r}
renv::init()
# Install Seurat v5.0.2 from GitHub
renv::install("satijalab/seurat@v5.0.2")
# Install the latest compatible SeuratObject (or specify version if needed)
#renv::install("satijalab/seurat-object")
```
```{r}
renv::snapshot()
```

## Initial Set Up
```{r}
setwd("/Users/queitsch/Documents/AdeyLab/1_Hybrid_Fusion/SmartSeq_CRC177_DeepSeq")
#/Users/queitsch/Documents/AdeyLab/1_Hybrid_Fusion/SmartSeqExp2_QC
```
# Set Seed
```{r}
set.seed(34)
```
# Load Libraries Required
```{r}
library(Seurat)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(clusterProfiler)
library(org.Hs.eg.db)
library(viridis)
library(ggrepel)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(tibble)
library(enrichplot)
```
## Process Raw Data for Each Library
# Process through default steps for CRC177 Tumor1
```{r}
counts<-read.delim(file="250306_smartseq3.bbrd.q10.filt.srt.counts.tsv")
CRC177Tumor1 <- CreateSeuratObject(counts = counts, project = "SmartDeep_CRC177Tumor1", min.features = 100)
CRC177Tumor1 <- NormalizeData(object = CRC177Tumor1)
CRC177Tumor1 <- FindVariableFeatures(object = CRC177Tumor1)
CRC177Tumor1 <- ScaleData(object = CRC177Tumor1)

dim(CRC177Tumor1) #27096    92
#It probably means that for 3 cells we have less than 100 features. 

CRC177Tumor1 <- RunPCA(object = CRC177Tumor1, npcs = 91) #One less than Column Value

CRC177Tumor1 <- FindNeighbors(object = CRC177Tumor1, dims = 1:91)
CRC177Tumor1 <- FindClusters(object = CRC177Tumor1)

#Run UMAP
CRC177Tumor1 <- RunUMAP(object = CRC177Tumor1, dims = 1:10)

#Plot UMAP
DimPlot(CRC177Tumor1, reduction = "umap") + plot_annotation(title = "CRC177Tumor1")

#Plot metadata
VlnPlot(CRC177Tumor1, features = c("nFeature_RNA","nCount_RNA")) + plot_annotation(title = "CRC177Tumor1")

# Add CellType metadata to the Seurat object
CRC177Tumor1$CellType <- "CRC177Tumor1"

# Verify that the metadata has been added
head(CRC177Tumor1@meta.data)
```
# Adding Metadata
```{r}
library(tibble)

# Add cell names as a column
CRC177Tumor1@meta.data <- rownames_to_column(CRC177Tumor1@meta.data, var = "cell_names_CRC177Tumor1")

# Read the well cell type data
well_celltype_data_CRC177Tumor1 <- read.csv("/Users/queitsch/Documents/AdeyLab/1_Hybrid_Fusion/SmartSeq_CRC177_DeepSeq/WellAnnotationFiles/WellAnnotation_Tumor_Plate3.csv")

# Merge the data frames
CRC177Tumor1@meta.data <- merge(CRC177Tumor1@meta.data, well_celltype_data_CRC177Tumor1, by.x = "cell_names_CRC177Tumor1", by.y = "CellID", all.x = TRUE)

# Convert the cell names column back to row names
CRC177Tumor1@meta.data <- column_to_rownames(CRC177Tumor1@meta.data, var = "cell_names_CRC177Tumor1")

# Check the result
head(CRC177Tumor1@meta.data)
```
##Updating Metadata for CHC vs. THC Analysis
```{r}
# Update metadata for cell type
CRC177Tumor1@meta.data$WellCellType[CRC177Tumor1@meta.data$WellCellType == "Hybrid"] <- "THC"
# Check the result
table(CRC177Tumor1@meta.data$WellCellType)
# Check the overall meta data structure is unchanged
head(CRC177Tumor1@meta.data)
```
```{r}
# Pull out Patient ID
CRC177Tumor1$PatientID <- "CRC177"
# Check the result
table(CRC177Tumor1@meta.data$PatientID)
# Check the overall meta data structure is unchanged
head(CRC177Tumor1@meta.data)
```

# Process through default steps for CRC177 PMBC1

```{r}
CRC177PBMC1 <- FindClusters(object = CRC177PBMC1)

# Add CellType metadata to the Seurat object
CRC177PBMC1$CellType <- "CRC177PBMC1"

# Verify that the metadata has been added
head(CRC177PBMC1@meta.data)
```

# PreMerge Trouble Shooting
```{r}
# Check metadata columns in each Seurat object
head(CRC177Tumor1@meta.data)
head(CRC177PBMC1@meta.data)
head(CRC177PBMC2@meta.data)
head(CRC176PBMC1@meta.data)
head(CRC176Tumor1@meta.data)
head(CRC179PBMC1@meta.data)
```
```{r}
sum(is.na(CRC177PBMC2$CellType))
sum(is.na(CRC177PBMC2$WellCellType))
sum(is.na(CRC177PBMC1$CellType))
sum(is.na(CRC177PBMC1$WellCellType))
sum(is.na(CRC176PBMC1$CellType))
sum(is.na(CRC176PBMC1$WellCellType))
sum(is.na(CRC179PBMC1$CellType))
sum(is.na(CRC179PBMC1$WellCellType))
sum(is.na(CRC177Tumor1$CellType))
sum(is.na(CRC177Tumor1$WellCellType))
sum(is.na(CRC176Tumor1$CellType))
sum(is.na(CRC176Tumor1$WellCellType))
```
```{r}
any(duplicated(colnames(CRC177Tumor1)))
any(duplicated(colnames(CRC177PBMC1)))
any(duplicated(colnames(CRC176PBMC1)))
any(duplicated(colnames(CRC177PBMC2)))
any(duplicated(colnames(CRC179PBMC1)))
any(duplicated(colnames(CRC176Tumor1)))
```

# Attempt to Merge
```{r}
# Merge the Seurat objects with unique cell names
merged_data_all <- merge(CRC177Tumor1, 
                     y = list(CRC177PBMC1, CRC177PBMC2, CRC176PBMC1, CRC176Tumor1, CRC179PBMC1), 
                     add.cell.ids = c("CRC177Tumor1", "CRC177PBMC1", "CRC177PBMC2", "CRC176PBMC1", "CRC176Tumor1", "CRC179PMBC1"))

# Verify the merged object
print(merged_data_all)
```

# Process Merged Seurat Object
```{r}
merged_data_all <- NormalizeData(object = merged_data_all) #Might not be required again
merged_data_all <- FindVariableFeatures(merged_data_all, selection.method = "vst", nfeatures = 2000) 
merged_data_all <- ScaleData(object = merged_data_all)
```
```{r}
dim(merged_data_all) #40287   539
# Check the dimensions of the scaled data matrix
```

# Run PCA
```{r}
# Run PCA
# Run PCA with a reduced number of PCs
merged_data_all <- RunPCA(merged_data_all, npcs = 50)  # Adjust npcs as needed
# Verify the PCA results
print(merged_data_all)
```
```{r}
# Check the variance explained by each PC
ElbowPlot(merged_data_all) #10 looks to be optimal
```
# Determine Clusters
```{r}
merged_data_all <- FindNeighbors(object = merged_data_all, dims = 1:10)
```
#Plot UMAP
```{r}
set.seed(48)
merged_data_all <- FindClusters(merged_data_all, resolution = 0.75) # Adjust the resolution value as needed
merged_data_all <- RunUMAP(merged_data_all, 
                          dims = 1:10, 
                          seed.use = 48,     # Explicit UMAP seed
                          verbose = TRUE)
DimPlot(merged_data_all, reduction = "umap", pt.size = 2, alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_color_viridis(discrete = TRUE) + plot_annotation(title = "0.75")


set.seed(48)
merged_data_all <- FindClusters(merged_data_all, resolution = 0.9) # Adjust the resolution value as needed. I decided on this, because it separates out the Cluster 5 - one of which ends up in CHC3 and one of which is largely composed of THC cells. 
merged_data_all <- RunUMAP(merged_data_all, 
                          dims = 1:10, 
                          seed.use = 48,     # Explicit UMAP seed
                          verbose = TRUE)
DimPlot(merged_data_all, reduction = "umap", pt.size = 2, alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_color_viridis(discrete = TRUE) + plot_annotation(title = "0.9")


```

#Save RDS of Seurat Object
```{r}
saveRDS(merged_data_all, file = "/Users/queitsch/Documents/AdeyLab/1_Hybrid_Fusion/SmartSeq_CRC177_DeepSeq/SmartSeqMerged.rds")
```

# Load in Merged Object
```{r}
merged_data_all_7 <- readRDS("/Users/queitsch/Documents/AdeyLab/1_Hybrid_Fusion/SmartSeq_CRC177_DeepSeq/SmartSeqMerged.rds")
```

# Basic UMAP Categorical Colors
```{r}
# Run UMAP
#merged_data_all_7 <- RunUMAP(object = merged_data_all_7, dims = 1:10)

# Create a custom palette that keeps most Set3 colors but replaces problematic ones
custom_colors <- c("#8DD3C7", "#FF6B6B", "#BEBADA", "#4CAF50", "#80B1D3",
                   "#FDB462", "#B3DE69", "#FCCDE5", "#D9D9D9", "#BC80BD",
                   "#CCEBC5", "#FFED6F")

DimPlot(merged_data_all_7, reduction = "umap", group.by = "seurat_clusters", pt.size = 2, alpha = 1) + theme(aspect.ratio = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  ggtitle("CRC Hybrid scRNA-seq SMART-seq")


# With Labels for CHC Grouping
custom_colors <- c("#8DD3C7", "#FF6B6B", "#BEBADA", "#4CAF50", "#80B1D3",
                   "#FDB462", "#B3DE69", "#FCCDE5", "#D9D9D9", "#BC80BD",
                   "#CCEBC5", "#FFED6F")

DimPlot(merged_data_all_7, reduction = "umap", group.by = "seurat_clusters", 
        pt.size = 2, alpha = 1, label = TRUE, label.size = 4) + 
  theme(aspect.ratio = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  ggtitle("CRC Hybrid scRNA-seq SMART-seq")
```

# UMAP by CellType
```{r}
DimPlot(merged_data_all_7, reduction = "umap", group.by = "CellType") + theme(aspect.ratio = 1) +
  scale_color_viridis(discrete = TRUE) + plot_annotation(title = "0.75 by Cell Type")
```
# UMAP by WellCellType
```{r}
# Plot UMAP with clusters associated with shapes
wellcell_plot <- DimPlot(merged_data_all_7, reduction = "umap", group.by = "WellCellType", pt.size = 2, alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_color_viridis(discrete = TRUE) + plot_annotation(title = "0.75 by Well Cell Type")


# Apply the viridis color scale and change the title
wellcell_plot + scale_color_viridis_d() + ggtitle("Well Cell Types by Cell Origin") +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL)
```
```{r}
# Define your custom colors
custom_colors <- c(
  "THC" = "red3",
  "CHC" = "pink2",
  "EpcamTumor" = "gray45",
  "CD45Immune" = "cadetblue3"
)

# Create the UMAP plot with custom colors
wellcell_plot <- DimPlot(
  merged_data_all_7,
  reduction = "umap",
  group.by = "WellCellType",
  pt.size = 2,
  alpha = 0.8
) +
  theme(aspect.ratio = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors) +
  ggtitle("Well Cell Types by Cell Origin") +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL)

# Display the plot
wellcell_plot

#Potentially we have 3 different, distinct transcriptomic profiles for CHCs, radiating out from the center.
```
# UMAP by Refined Cell Type
```{r}
# Create refined cell type classification with CHC subgroups
# Based on manual cluster assignments for CHC cells
# First, let's create the new metadata column
merged_data_all_7$RefinedCellType <- merged_data_all_7$WellCellType

# Update CHC cells based on their cluster assignments
# CHC1: Cluster 1
merged_data_all_7$RefinedCellType[merged_data_all_7$WellCellType == "CHC" &
                                  merged_data_all_7$seurat_clusters == 1] <- "CHC1"
# CHC2: Clusters 2, 4  6
merged_data_all_7$RefinedCellType[merged_data_all_7$WellCellType == "CHC" &
                                  merged_data_all_7$seurat_clusters %in% c(2, 4, 6)] <- "CHC2"
# CHC3: Clusters 0, 8, 9
merged_data_all_7$RefinedCellType[merged_data_all_7$WellCellType == "CHC" &
                                  merged_data_all_7$seurat_clusters %in% c(0, 8, 9)] <- "CHC3"

# # Update THC cells based on their cluster assignments
# # THC1: Cluster 7
# merged_data_all_7$RefinedCellType[merged_data_all_7$WellCellType == "THC" &
#                                   merged_data_all_7$seurat_clusters == 7] <- "THC1"
# # THC2: Clusters 10
# merged_data_all_7$RefinedCellType[merged_data_all_7$WellCellType == "THC" &
#                                   merged_data_all_7$seurat_clusters %in% 10] <- "THC2"
# # THC3: Clusters 11
# merged_data_all_7$RefinedCellType[merged_data_all_7$WellCellType == "THC" &
#                                   merged_data_all_7$seurat_clusters %in% 11] <- "THC3"
# I decided against including THC subgroups - though we see two small sateillite clusters 10 and 11 with THCs - they're only comprised of 10 cells each. It ignores the THCs that cluster w/ CHC2 and the EpcamTumors in the middle


#CD45Immune        CHC       CHC1       CHC2       CHC3 EpcamTumor        THC       THC1 
#        82         11         55        140        100         30         65         36 
#      THC2       THC3 
#        10         10 

# Verify the classification
print("Cell counts by RefinedCellType:")
table(merged_data_all_7$RefinedCellType)
# Cross-tabulation to verify CHC subgroup assignments
print("\nCross-tabulation of RefinedCellType vs Clusters (CHC cells only):")
chc_cells <- merged_data_all_7$WellCellType == "CHC"
table(merged_data_all_7$RefinedCellType[chc_cells],
      merged_data_all_7$seurat_clusters[chc_cells])
```

```{r}
# Define updated custom colors with CHC subgroups

  "THC" = "red3",
  "CHC1" = "pink1",
  "CHC2" = "pink2",
  "CHC3" = "pink3",
  "EpcamTumor" = "gray45",
  "CD45Immune" = "cadetblue3"
)

# Create the refined UMAP plot

  merged_data_all_7,
  reduction = "umap",
  group.by = "RefinedCellType",
  pt.size = 2,
  alpha = 0.8
) +
  theme(aspect.ratio = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors_refined) +

        legend.text = element_text(size = 10))

# Optional: Create a side-by-side comparison

# Original plot (for comparison)

  merged_data_all_7,
  reduction = "umap",
  group.by = "WellCellType",
  pt.size = 2,
  alpha = 0.8
) +
  theme(aspect.ratio = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("THC" = "red3", "CHC" = "pink2",
                               "EpcamTumor" = "gray45", "CD45Immune" = "cadetblue3")) +
  ggtitle("Original Cell Types") +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL)

# Side-by-side comparison
comparison_plot <- original_plot | refined_wellcell_plot
comparison_plot
```

```{r}
# First get all unique cluster levels to ensure proper ordering
all_clusters <- sort(unique(as.numeric(merged_data_all_7$seurat_clusters)))
custom_colors <- c("#8DD3C7", "#FF6B6B", "#BEBADA", "#4CAF50", "#80B1D3",

# Create default seurat clusters plot for comparison WITH LABELS

        axis.line = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks = element_blank()) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  ggtitle("Default Seurat Clusters")

default_cluster_plot

# Create annotated cluster labels
merged_data_all_7$annotated_clusters <- as.character(merged_data_all_7$seurat_clusters)

# Update cluster labels with CHC annotations
merged_data_all_7$annotated_clusters[merged_data_all_7$seurat_clusters == 1] <- "1.CHC1"
merged_data_all_7$annotated_clusters[merged_data_all_7$seurat_clusters == 2] <- "2.CHC2"
merged_data_all_7$annotated_clusters[merged_data_all_7$seurat_clusters == 4] <- "4.CHC2"
merged_data_all_7$annotated_clusters[merged_data_all_7$seurat_clusters == 6] <- "6.CHC2"
merged_data_all_7$annotated_clusters[merged_data_all_7$seurat_clusters == 0] <- "0.CHC3"
merged_data_all_7$annotated_clusters[merged_data_all_7$seurat_clusters == 8] <- "8.CHC3"
merged_data_all_7$annotated_clusters[merged_data_all_7$seurat_clusters == 9] <- "9.CHC3"

# Create colors for annotated clusters
# Define CHC colors
chc1_color <- rgb(236/255, 164/255, 0/255)
chc2_color <- rgb(40/255, 0/255, 3/255)
chc3_color <- rgb(30/255, 145/255, 214/255)

# # Create color mapping for annotated clusters
# annotated_colors <- c(
#   "0.CHC3" = chc3_color,
#   "1.CHC1" = chc1_color,
#   "2.CHC2" = chc2_color,
#   "4.CHC2" = chc2_color,
#   "6.CHC2" = chc2_color,
#   "8.CHC3" = chc3_color,
#   "9.CHC3" = chc3_color
# )

# Create color mapping for annotated clusters with scaled intensities
library(scales)

# Function to create lighter versions of colors
lighten_color <- function(color, amount = 0.5) {
  # Convert to RGB, blend with white, convert back
  col_rgb <- col2rgb(color)
  white_rgb <- col2rgb("white")
  new_rgb <- col_rgb + amount * (white_rgb - col_rgb)
  rgb(new_rgb[1,], new_rgb[2,], new_rgb[3,], maxColorValue = 255)
}

# Create scaled versions of each CHC color
# CHC3 colors (35%, 75%, 100%)
chc3_55 <- lighten_color(chc3_color, 0.45)  # 65% lighter = 35% intensity
chc3_75 <- lighten_color(chc3_color, 0.25)  # 25% lighter = 75% intensity
chc3_100 <- chc3_color                      # Full intensity

# CHC2 colors (35%, 75%, 100%)
chc2_55 <- lighten_color(chc2_color, 0.45)
chc2_75 <- lighten_color(chc2_color, 0.25)
chc2_100 <- chc2_color

annotated_colors <- c(
  "0.CHC3" = chc3_55,   # 35% intensity
  "1.CHC1" = chc1_color, # Keep original
  "2.CHC2" = chc2_55,   # 35% intensity
  "4.CHC2" = chc2_75,   # 75% intensity
  "6.CHC2" = chc2_100,  # 100% intensity
  "8.CHC3" = chc3_75,   # 75% intensity
  "9.CHC3" = chc3_100   # 100% intensity
)


# Add colors for any remaining clusters that aren't CHC
remaining_clusters <- setdiff(unique(merged_data_all_7$annotated_clusters), names(annotated_colors))
for(i in seq_along(remaining_clusters)) {
  cluster_num <- as.numeric(gsub("\\..*", "", remaining_clusters[i]))
  annotated_colors[remaining_clusters[i]] <- custom_colors[cluster_num + 1]
}

# Debug: Print the color mapping
print("Color mapping for annotated clusters:")
print(annotated_colors)

# Ensure all annotated cluster levels are included in colors
all_annotated_levels <- unique(merged_data_all_7$annotated_clusters)
print("All annotated cluster levels present:")
print(all_annotated_levels)

# Check if any levels are missing from color mapping
missing_colors <- setdiff(all_annotated_levels, names(annotated_colors))
if(length(missing_colors) > 0) {
  print("Missing colors for these levels:")
  print(missing_colors)
  # Add missing colors
  for(missing in missing_colors) {
    annotated_colors[missing] <- "gray50"  # Default gray for missing
  }
}

# Set the correct order for annotated clusters
correct_order <- c("0.CHC3", "1.CHC1", "2.CHC2", "3", "4.CHC2", "5", "6.CHC2", "7", "8.CHC3", "9.CHC3", "10", "11")

# Convert to factor with the correct level order
merged_data_all_7$annotated_clusters <- factor(merged_data_all_7$annotated_clusters, levels = correct_order)

# Define colors for refined cell types
refined_colors <- c(
  "THC" = "red3",
  "CHC1" = chc1_color,
  "CHC2" = chc2_color,
  "CHC3" = chc3_color,
  "EpcamTumor" = "gray45",
  "CD45Immune" = "cadetblue3"
)

# Plot UMAP by annotated clusters
cluster_plot <- DimPlot(merged_data_all_7,
                       reduction = "umap",
                       group.by = "annotated_clusters",
                       pt.size = 2,
                       alpha = 1) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(aspect.ratio = 1,
        text = element_text(size = 6),
        axis.text = element_blank(),
        axis.title = element_blank(),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 6),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 6),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 6),
        axis.line = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks = element_blank()) +
  scale_color_manual(values = annotated_colors) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  ggtitle("CRC Hybrid scRNA-seq SMART-seq (Clusters)")

# Plot UMAP by refined cell types
refined_plot <- DimPlot(merged_data_all_7,
                       reduction = "umap",
                       group.by = "RefinedCellType",
                       pt.size = 2,
                       alpha = 1) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(aspect.ratio = 1,
        text = element_text(size = 6),
        axis.text = element_blank(),
        axis.title = element_blank(),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 6),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 6),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 6),
        axis.line = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks = element_blank()) +
  scale_color_manual(values = refined_colors) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  ggtitle("CRC Hybrid scRNA-seq SMART-seq (Clusters Grouped)")



# Debug: Check what's happening with cluster 0
print("Unique values in seurat_clusters:")
print(sort(unique(merged_data_all_7$seurat_clusters)))

print("Unique values in annotated_clusters:")
print(sort(unique(merged_data_all_7$annotated_clusters)))

print("Check cluster 0 specifically:")

print(paste("Number of cells in cluster 0:", sum(cluster_0_cells)))
print("Annotated cluster values for cluster 0 cells:")

# Display plots
default_cluster_plot
cluster_plot

# Create side-by-side comparison
library(patchwork)

# Save plots
pdf("250725.Fig7Final/250725.PanelB.cluster_umap.pdf", width = 6, height = 5)
```

```{r}
# Create a CHC grouping variable (if you haven't already)
merged_data_all_7$CHC_group <- "Other"
merged_data_all_7$CHC_group[grepl("CHC1", merged_data_all_7$annotated_clusters)] <- "CHC1"
merged_data_all_7$CHC_group[grepl("CHC2", merged_data_all_7$annotated_clusters)] <- "CHC2"
merged_data_all_7$CHC_group[grepl("CHC3", merged_data_all_7$annotated_clusters)] <- "CHC3"

# Get UMAP coordinates
umap_coords <- as.data.frame(merged_data_all_7@reductions$umap@cell.embeddings)
umap_coords$CHC_group <- merged_data_all_7$CHC_group

# Add ellipses to your existing cluster_plot
cluster_plot_with_ellipses <- cluster_plot +
  stat_ellipse(data = subset(umap_coords, CHC_group == "CHC1"),
               aes(x = umap_1, y = umap_2),
               color = "black", size = 1.5, linetype = "solid", inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  stat_ellipse(data = subset(umap_coords, CHC_group == "CHC2"),
               aes(x = umap_1, y = umap_2),
               color = "black", size = 1.5, linetype = "solid", inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  stat_ellipse(data = subset(umap_coords, CHC_group == "CHC3"),
               aes(x = umap_1, y = umap_2),
               color = "black", size = 1.5, linetype = "solid", inherit.aes = FALSE)

# Display the plot
cluster_plot_with_ellipses

# Save the new version
pdf("250725.Fig7Final/PanelB.cluster_umap_with_ellipsesp.pdf", width = 6, height = 5)
cluster_plot_with_ellipses
dev.off()
png("250725.Fig7Final/250725.PanelB.cluster_umap_with_ellipses.png", width = 6, height = 5, units = "in", res = 300)
cluster_plot_with_ellipses
dev.off()
```

```{r}
})

# Display the combined plot
combined_plot
```
# Violin Plot for Specific Genes of Interest Expression by WellCellType, Better Aspect Ratio, Matched Colors, and T-Test
```{r}
# Define your custom colors
custom_colors <- c(
  "THC" = "red3",
  "CHC" = "pink2",
  "EpcamTumor" = "gray45",
  "CD45Immune" = "cadetblue3"
)

# Function to perform t-tests comparing each cell type to EpcamTumor
perform_statistical_tests <- function(seurat_object, gene_name, group_column = "WellCellType") {
  # Extract expression data for the gene
  gene_data <- FetchData(seurat_object, vars = c(gene_name, group_column))

  # Get unique cell types (excluding EpcamTumor as it's our reference)
  cell_types <- unique(gene_data[[group_column]])
  cell_types_to_test <- cell_types[cell_types != "EpcamTumor"]

  # Store results
  test_results <- list()

  # Perform t-tests for each cell type vs EpcamTumor
  for (cell_type in cell_types_to_test) {
    # Subset data for current cell type and EpcamTumor
    subset_data <- gene_data %>%
      filter(!!sym(group_column) %in% c(cell_type, "EpcamTumor"))

    # Perform t-test
    if (nrow(subset_data) > 0 && length(unique(subset_data[[group_column]])) == 2) {
      t_test_result <- t.test(
        formula = as.formula(paste(gene_name, "~", group_column)),
        data = subset_data
      )

      test_results[[paste(cell_type, "vs EpcamTumor")]] <- list(
        comparison = paste(cell_type, "vs EpcamTumor"),
        p_value = t_test_result$p.value,
        statistic = t_test_result$statistic,
        mean_diff = diff(t_test_result$estimate),
        significant = t_test_result$p.value < 0.05
      )
    }
  }

  return(test_results)
}

# Create square-shaped violin plots with consistent size and custom colors
vln_plots <- lapply(seq_along(genes_of_interest), function(i) {
  gene <- genes_of_interest[i]

  # Perform statistical tests for this gene
  stats_results <- perform_statistical_tests(merged_data_all_7, gene)

  # Print statistical results for this gene
  cat("\n=== Statistical Results for", gene, "===\n")
  for (comparison_name in names(stats_results)) {
    result <- stats_results[[comparison_name]]
    cat(sprintf("%s: p-value = %.4f (%s)\n",
                result$comparison, result$p_value,
                ifelse(result$significant, "significant", "not significant")))
  }

  # Create the base plot
  p <- VlnPlot(
    merged_data_all_7,
    features = gene,
    group.by = "WellCellType",
    pt.size = 0.1,
    slot = "data",
    cols = custom_colors  # Apply custom colors
  ) +
    ylim(0, 5.5) +  # Increased ylim to accommodate significance annotations
    ggtitle(gene) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 14),
      plot.margin = margin(5, 5, 5, 5)  # minimize padding
    )

  # Add significance annotations overlaid on the plot
  # Get cell type order from the plot (assuming standard order)
  cell_types <- c("THC", "CHC", "EpcamTumor", "CD45Immune")
  epcam_position <- which(cell_types == "EpcamTumor")

  # Create annotation data frame
  annotation_data <- data.frame()
  y_position <- 5.2  # Position for significance annotations

  for (comparison_name in names(stats_results)) {
    result <- stats_results[[comparison_name]]
    # Extract cell type from comparison name
    cell_type <- strsplit(comparison_name, " vs ")[[1]][1]
    cell_position <- which(cell_types == cell_type)

    # Determine significance symbol
    sig_symbol <- if (result$p_value < 0.001) "***"
                 else if (result$p_value < 0.01) "**"
                 else if (result$p_value < 0.05) "*"
                 else "ns"

    # Only add annotation if significant (remove "ns" if you want to show all)
    if (sig_symbol != "ns") {
      annotation_data <- rbind(annotation_data, data.frame(
        x = cell_position,
        y = y_position,
        label = sig_symbol,
        cell_type = cell_type
      ))
    }
  }

  # Add significance annotations to the plot
  if (nrow(annotation_data) > 0) {
    p <- p +
      geom_text(data = annotation_data,
                aes(x = x, y = y, label = label),
                inherit.aes = FALSE,
                size = 4,
                fontface = "bold",
                color = "black")
  }

  # Keep legend only for first plot
  if (i > 1) p <- p + NoLegend()
  return(p)
})

# Arrange in 2x3 grid and collect legend
combined_vln_plot <- wrap_plots(vln_plots, ncol = 3) +
                     plot_layout(guides = "collect")

# Display
combined_vln_plot

# Optional: Create a summary table of all statistical results
create_stats_summary <- function(genes_list, seurat_object) {
  all_results <- data.frame()

  for (gene in genes_list) {
    stats_results <- perform_statistical_tests(seurat_object, gene)

    for (comparison_name in names(stats_results)) {
      result <- stats_results[[comparison_name]]
      all_results <- rbind(all_results, data.frame(
        Gene = gene,
        Comparison = result$comparison,
        P_value = result$p_value,
        T_statistic = result$statistic,
        Mean_difference = result$mean_diff,
        Significant = result$significant,
        Significance_level = ifelse(result$p_value < 0.001, "***",
                                  ifelse(result$p_value < 0.01, "**",
                                        ifelse(result$p_value < 0.05, "*", "ns")))
      ))
    }
  }

  return(all_results)
}

# Create and display summary table
stats_summary <- create_stats_summary(genes_of_interest, merged_data_all_7)
print(stats_summary)
```

```{r}
merged_data_all_7 <- readRDS("/Users/queitsch/Documents/AdeyLab/1_Hybrid_Fusion/SmartSeq_CRC177_DeepSeq/SmartSeqMerged.rds")
```

# Panel F: Updated Colors, CHC Subset Breakdown
```{r}
# Load required libraries (add these if not already loaded)
# library(Seurat)
# library(ggplot2)
# library(patchwork)
# library(dplyr)

# Define updated custom colors with CHC subgroups
custom_colors_refined <- c(
  "THC" = "red3",
  "CHC" = "pink1",
  "CHC1" = rgb(236/255, 164/255, 0/255),
  "CHC2" = rgb(40/255, 0/255, 3/255),
  "CHC3" = rgb(30/255, 145/255, 214/255), #"CHC3" = rgb(121/255, 30/255, 148/255),
  "EpcamTumor" = "gray45",
  "CD45Immune" = "cadetblue3"
)

# Function to calculate Cliff's delta (effect size for non-parametric tests)
calculate_cliff_delta <- function(group1, group2) {
  n1 <- length(group1)
  n2 <- length(group2)
  
  # Create all pairwise comparisons
  comparisons <- outer(group1, group2, function(x, y) {
    ifelse(x > y, 1, ifelse(x < y, -1, 0))
  })
  
  # Cliff's delta = (number of pairs where group1 > group2 - number where group1 < group2) / total pairs
  cliff_delta <- sum(comparisons) / (n1 * n2)
  return(cliff_delta)
}

# Function to interpret Cliff's delta effect size
interpret_cliff_delta <- function(delta) {
  abs_delta <- abs(delta)
  if (abs_delta < 0.147) return("negligible")
  else if (abs_delta < 0.33) return("small")
  else if (abs_delta < 0.474) return("medium")
  else return("large")
}

# Simplified function to perform Wilcoxon tests only (skip Friedman for now)
perform_wilcoxon_tests <- function(seurat_object, gene_name, group_column = "RefinedCellType") {
  
  cat(sprintf("\n=== Analyzing %s ===\n", gene_name))
  
  # Extract expression data for the gene
  gene_data <- FetchData(seurat_object, vars = c(gene_name, group_column))
  
  # Remove CHC from the analysis
  gene_data <- gene_data[gene_data[[group_column]] != "CHC", ]
  
  # Get unique cell types (excluding EpcamTumor as it's our reference, and CHC which we're removing)
  cell_types <- unique(gene_data[[group_column]])
  cell_types_to_test <- cell_types[cell_types != "EpcamTumor" & cell_types != "CHC"]
  
  cat(sprintf("Cell types found (excluding CHC): %s\n", paste(cell_types, collapse = ", ")))
  cat(sprintf("Testing against EpcamTumor: %s\n", paste(cell_types_to_test, collapse = ", ")))
  
  # Store results
  test_results <- list()
  
  # Perform Wilcoxon tests for each cell type vs EpcamTumor
  for (cell_type in cell_types_to_test) {
    cat(sprintf("\nTesting %s vs EpcamTumor...\n", cell_type))
    
    # Subset data for current cell type and EpcamTumor
    subset_data <- gene_data[gene_data[[group_column]] %in% c(cell_type, "EpcamTumor"), ]
    
    # Check if we have both groups
    if (nrow(subset_data) > 0 && length(unique(subset_data[[group_column]])) == 2) {
      # Get the two groups
      group1_data <- subset_data[subset_data[[group_column]] == cell_type, gene_name]
      group2_data <- subset_data[subset_data[[group_column]] == "EpcamTumor", gene_name]
      
      # Remove any NA values
      group1_data <- group1_data[!is.na(group1_data)]
      group2_data <- group2_data[!is.na(group2_data)]
      
      if (length(group1_data) > 0 && length(group2_data) > 0) {
        # Perform Wilcoxon test
        wilcox_result <- wilcox.test(group1_data, group2_data, 
                                     alternative = "two.sided", 
                                     exact = FALSE)
        
        # Calculate effect size (Cliff's delta)
        cliff_delta <- calculate_cliff_delta(group1_data, group2_data)
        
        # Calculate medians for interpretation
        median1 <- median(group1_data, na.rm = TRUE)
        median2 <- median(group2_data, na.rm = TRUE)
        
        test_results[[paste(cell_type, "vs EpcamTumor")]] <- list(
          comparison = paste(cell_type, "vs EpcamTumor"),
          p_value = wilcox_result$p.value,
          statistic = wilcox_result$statistic,
          median_diff = median1 - median2,
          median_celltype = median1,
          median_epcam = median2,
          cliff_delta = cliff_delta,
          effect_size_interpretation = interpret_cliff_delta(cliff_delta),
          significant = wilcox_result$p.value < 0.05,
          n_celltype = length(group1_data),
          n_epcam = length(group2_data)
        )
        
        # Print results immediately
        cat(sprintf("  p-value: %.4f (%s)\n", wilcox_result$p.value, 
                    ifelse(wilcox_result$p.value < 0.05, "significant", "not significant")))
        cat(sprintf("  Medians: %.3f vs %.3f (diff: %.3f)\n", median1, median2, median1 - median2))
        cat(sprintf("  Effect size: %.3f (%s)\n", cliff_delta, interpret_cliff_delta(cliff_delta)))
        cat(sprintf("  Sample sizes: %d vs %d\n", length(group1_data), length(group2_data)))
      } else {
        cat(sprintf("  Skipped - insufficient data after removing NAs\n"))
      }
    } else {
      cat(sprintf("  Skipped - missing one or both groups\n"))
    }
  }
  
  return(test_results)
}

# Function to apply multiple testing correction
apply_multiple_testing_correction <- function(test_results, method = "BH") {
  if (length(test_results) == 0) return(test_results)
  
  # Extract p-values
  p_values <- sapply(test_results, function(x) x$p_value)
  
  # Apply correction
  adjusted_p <- p.adjust(p_values, method = method)
  
  # Update results with adjusted p-values
  for (i in seq_along(test_results)) {
    test_results[[i]]$p_value_adjusted <- adjusted_p[i]
    test_results[[i]]$significant_adjusted <- adjusted_p[i] < 0.05
  }
  
  return(test_results)
}

# Create output directory if it doesn't exist
if (!dir.exists("250725.Fig7Final")) {
  dir.create("250725.Fig7Final", recursive = TRUE)
  cat("Created directory: 250725.Fig7Final\n")
}

# Test with first gene to make sure everything works
cat("Testing with first gene to verify setup...\n")
if (exists("genes_of_interest") && length(genes_of_interest) > 0) {
  test_gene <- genes_of_interest[1]
  cat(sprintf("Test gene: %s\n", test_gene))
  
  # Test the analysis function
  test_results <- perform_wilcoxon_tests(merged_data_all_7, test_gene)
  
  if (length(test_results) > 0) {
    cat("\nAnalysis function working! Proceeding with all genes...\n")
    
    # Now run the full analysis
    all_results <- list()
    
    # Create plots for each gene
    vln_plots_refined <- list()
    
    for (i in seq_along(genes_of_interest)) {
      gene <- genes_of_interest[i]
      cat(sprintf("\n### Processing gene %d/%d: %s ###\n", i, length(genes_of_interest), gene))
      
      # Perform statistical tests
      stats_results <- perform_wilcoxon_tests(merged_data_all_7, gene)
      
      # Apply multiple testing correction
      if (length(stats_results) > 0) {
        stats_results <- apply_multiple_testing_correction(stats_results, method = "BH")
        all_results[[gene]] <- stats_results
        
        cat("\nAfter multiple testing correction:\n")
        for (comparison_name in names(stats_results)) {
          result <- stats_results[[comparison_name]]
          cat(sprintf("%s: raw p=%.4f, adj p=%.4f (%s)\n", 
                      result$comparison, result$p_value, result$p_value_adjusted,
                      ifelse(result$significant_adjusted, "significant", "not significant")))
        }
      }
      
      # Create violin plot
      cat("Creating violin plot...\n")
      
      tryCatch({
        # First create a basic plot without annotations, excluding CHC
        # Subset the data to exclude CHC
        subset_obj <- subset(merged_data_all_7, subset = RefinedCellType != "CHC")
        
        p <- VlnPlot(
          subset_obj,
          features = gene,
          group.by = "RefinedCellType",
          pt.size = 0.1,
          slot = "data",
          cols = custom_colors_refined
        ) +
          ggtitle(gene) +
          theme_minimal(base_size = 6) +  # Set base text size to 6
          theme(
            axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 6),
            axis.text.y = element_text(size = 6),
            axis.title.x = element_text(size = 6),
            axis.title.y = element_text(size = 6),
            plot.title = element_text(size = 6),
            legend.text = element_text(size = 6),
            legend.title = element_text(size = 6),
            plot.margin = margin(5, 5, 5, 5)
          )
        
        cat("Basic violin plot created successfully (CHC excluded).\n")
        
        # Add significance annotations if we have results
        if (length(stats_results) > 0) {
          cat("Adding significance annotations...\n")
          
          # Get the plot build to access the exact x-axis positions
          plot_build <- ggplot_build(p)
          
          # Extract x-axis labels and their positions from the built plot
          x_labels <- plot_build$layout$panel_params[[1]]$x$get_labels()
          x_positions <- 1:length(x_labels)
          
          cat(sprintf("Plot x-axis order: %s\n", paste(x_labels, collapse = ", ")))
          cat(sprintf("Positions: %s\n", paste(x_positions, collapse = ", ")))
          
          # Get expression data for y-positioning
          plot_data <- FetchData(subset_obj, vars = c(gene, "RefinedCellType"))
          max_expression <- max(plot_data[[1]], na.rm = TRUE)
          min_expression <- min(plot_data[[1]], na.rm = TRUE)
          y_annotation <- min_expression + (max_expression - min_expression) * 0.5  # Middle of the plot
          
          cat(sprintf("Expression range: %.3f to %.3f, annotation y-position: %.3f\n", 
                      min_expression, max_expression, y_annotation))
          
          # Create annotations based on the actual plot order
          sig_annotations <- data.frame()
          
          for (comparison_name in names(stats_results)) {
            result <- stats_results[[comparison_name]]
            cell_type <- strsplit(comparison_name, " vs ")[[1]][1]
            
            # Find the exact position in the plot
            if (cell_type %in% x_labels) {
              cell_position <- which(x_labels == cell_type)
              
              # Determine significance
              if (result$p_value_adjusted < 0.001) {
                sig_symbol <- "***"
              } else if (result$p_value_adjusted < 0.01) {
                sig_symbol <- "**"
              } else if (result$p_value_adjusted < 0.05) {
                sig_symbol <- "*"
              } else {
                sig_symbol <- NULL  # Don't annotate non-significant
              }
              
              # Add to annotations if significant
              if (!is.null(sig_symbol)) {
                sig_annotations <- rbind(sig_annotations, data.frame(
                  x = cell_position,
                  y = y_annotation,
                  label = sig_symbol,
                  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
                ))
                
                cat(sprintf("  %s: %s (plot position %d)\n", cell_type, sig_symbol, cell_position))
              }
            } else {
              cat(sprintf("  %s: not found in plot x-axis\n", cell_type))
            }
          }
          
          # Add annotations to plot if any exist
          if (nrow(sig_annotations) > 0) {
            p <- p +
              # First add black outline (slightly larger)
              annotate("text", 
                       x = sig_annotations$x, 
                       y = sig_annotations$y, 
                       label = sig_annotations$label,
                       size = 4.5,  # Slightly larger for outline effect
                       fontface = "bold",
                       color = "white") +
              # Then add white text on top
              annotate("text", 
                       x = sig_annotations$x, 
                       y = sig_annotations$y, 
                       label = sig_annotations$label,
                       size = 4,  # One size bigger than before (was 3)
                       fontface = "bold",
                       color = "black")
            
            cat(sprintf("Added %d significance annotations in middle of plot\n", nrow(sig_annotations)))
          } else {
            cat("No significant results to annotate\n")
          }
        }
        
        cat("Final plot prepared successfully.\n")
        
        vln_plots_refined[[i]] <- p
        
        # Save individual plot
        ggsave(filename = sprintf("250725.Fig7Final/violin_plot_%s.png", gene),
               plot = p, width = 4, height = 4, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
        ggsave(filename = sprintf("250725.Fig7Final/violin_plot_%s.pdf", gene),
               plot = p, width = 4, height = 4, bg = "white")
        
        cat("Violin plot created and saved successfully!\n")
        
      }, error = function(e) {
        cat(sprintf("Error creating plot for %s: %s\n", gene, e$message))
        vln_plots_refined[[i]] <- NULL  # Set to NULL if plot creation fails
      })
    }
    
    # Create combined plot if we have individual plots
    if (length(vln_plots_refined) > 0) {
      cat("\nCreating combined plot...\n")
      
      # Filter out any NULL plots
      valid_plots <- vln_plots_refined[!sapply(vln_plots_refined, is.null)]
      
      if (length(valid_plots) > 0) {
        tryCatch({
          combined_plot <- wrap_plots(valid_plots, ncol = 3)
          
          # Save combined plot
          ggsave(filename = "250725.Fig7Final/violin_plots_combined.png",
                 plot = combined_plot, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
          ggsave(filename = "250725.Fig7Final/violin_plots_combined.pdf",
                 plot = combined_plot, width = 12, height = 8, bg = "white")
          
          cat(sprintf("Combined plot saved successfully with %d plots!\n", length(valid_plots)))
          
          # Display the combined plot
          print(combined_plot)
          
        }, error = function(e) {
          cat(sprintf("Error creating combined plot: %s\n", e$message))
        })
      } else {
        cat("No valid plots to combine.\n")
      }
    }
    
    cat("\n=== ANALYSIS COMPLETE ===\n")
    cat("Files saved to 250725.Fig7Final/\n")
    cat("- Individual plots: violin_plot_[GENE].png/pdf\n")
    cat("- Combined plot: violin_plots_combined.png/pdf\n")
    
  } else {
    cat("No results from test analysis. Please check your data and gene names.\n")
  }
} else {
  cat("Error: genes_of_interest not found or empty. Please define this variable first.\n")
  cat("Example: genes_of_interest <- c('GENE1', 'GENE2', 'GENE3')\n")
}
```
# Panel F: 250730 - new CH3 color
```{r}
# Load required libraries (add these if not already loaded)
# library(Seurat)
# library(ggplot2)
# library(patchwork)
# library(dplyr)

# Define updated custom colors with CHC subgroups
custom_colors_refined <- c(
  "THC" = "red3",
  "CHC" = "pink1",
  "CHC1" = rgb(236/255, 164/255, 0/255),
  "CHC2" = rgb(40/255, 0/255, 3/255),
  "CHC3" = rgb(30/255, 145/255, 214/255), #"CHC3" = rgb(121/255, 30/255, 148/255),
  "EpcamTumor" = "gray45",
  "CD45Immune" = "cadetblue3"
)

# EXPLICITLY DEFINE THE GENE LIST IN THE CORRECT ORDER
genes_of_interest <- c("RUNX1", "THBS1", "SPI1", "GRN", "PF4", "LGALS3")

# Function to calculate Cliff's delta (effect size for non-parametric tests)
calculate_cliff_delta <- function(group1, group2) {
  n1 <- length(group1)
  n2 <- length(group2)
  
  # Create all pairwise comparisons
  comparisons <- outer(group1, group2, function(x, y) {
    ifelse(x > y, 1, ifelse(x < y, -1, 0))
  })
  
  # Cliff's delta = (number of pairs where group1 > group2 - number where group1 < group2) / total pairs
  cliff_delta <- sum(comparisons) / (n1 * n2)
  return(cliff_delta)
}

# Function to interpret Cliff's delta effect size
interpret_cliff_delta <- function(delta) {
  abs_delta <- abs(delta)
  if (abs_delta < 0.147) return("negligible")
  else if (abs_delta < 0.33) return("small")
  else if (abs_delta < 0.474) return("medium")
  else return("large")
}

# Simplified function to perform Wilcoxon tests only (skip Friedman for now)
perform_wilcoxon_tests <- function(seurat_object, gene_name, group_column = "RefinedCellType") {
  
  cat(sprintf("\n=== Analyzing %s ===\n", gene_name))
  
  # Check if gene exists in the dataset
  if (!gene_name %in% rownames(seurat_object)) {
    cat(sprintf("WARNING: Gene %s not found in dataset. Available genes that start with '%s':\n", 
                gene_name, substr(gene_name, 1, 3)))
    matching_genes <- grep(paste0("^", substr(gene_name, 1, 3)), rownames(seurat_object), value = TRUE)
    if (length(matching_genes) > 0) {
      cat(paste(head(matching_genes, 10), collapse = ", "))
      if (length(matching_genes) > 10) cat(" ... and more")
      cat("\n")
    } else {
      cat("No similar genes found.\n")
    }
    return(list())
  }
  
  # Extract expression data for the gene
  gene_data <- FetchData(seurat_object, vars = c(gene_name, group_column))
  
  # Remove CHC from the analysis
  gene_data <- gene_data[gene_data[[group_column]] != "CHC", ]
  
  # Get unique cell types (excluding EpcamTumor as it's our reference, and CHC which we're removing)
  cell_types <- unique(gene_data[[group_column]])
  cell_types_to_test <- cell_types[cell_types != "EpcamTumor" & cell_types != "CHC"]
  
  cat(sprintf("Cell types found (excluding CHC): %s\n", paste(cell_types, collapse = ", ")))
  cat(sprintf("Testing against EpcamTumor: %s\n", paste(cell_types_to_test, collapse = ", ")))
  
  # Store results
  test_results <- list()
  
  # Perform Wilcoxon tests for each cell type vs EpcamTumor
  for (cell_type in cell_types_to_test) {
    cat(sprintf("\nTesting %s vs EpcamTumor...\n", cell_type))
    
    # Subset data for current cell type and EpcamTumor
    subset_data <- gene_data[gene_data[[group_column]] %in% c(cell_type, "EpcamTumor"), ]
    
    # Check if we have both groups
    if (nrow(subset_data) > 0 && length(unique(subset_data[[group_column]])) == 2) {
      # Get the two groups
      group1_data <- subset_data[subset_data[[group_column]] == cell_type, gene_name]
      group2_data <- subset_data[subset_data[[group_column]] == "EpcamTumor", gene_name]
      
      # Remove any NA values
      group1_data <- group1_data[!is.na(group1_data)]
      group2_data <- group2_data[!is.na(group2_data)]
      
      if (length(group1_data) > 0 && length(group2_data) > 0) {
        # Perform Wilcoxon test
        wilcox_result <- wilcox.test(group1_data, group2_data, 
                                     alternative = "two.sided", 
                                     exact = FALSE)
        
        # Calculate effect size (Cliff's delta)
        cliff_delta <- calculate_cliff_delta(group1_data, group2_data)
        
        # Calculate medians for interpretation
        median1 <- median(group1_data, na.rm = TRUE)
        median2 <- median(group2_data, na.rm = TRUE)
        
        test_results[[paste(cell_type, "vs EpcamTumor")]] <- list(
          comparison = paste(cell_type, "vs EpcamTumor"),
          p_value = wilcox_result$p.value,
          statistic = wilcox_result$statistic,
          median_diff = median1 - median2,
          median_celltype = median1,
          median_epcam = median2,
          cliff_delta = cliff_delta,
          effect_size_interpretation = interpret_cliff_delta(cliff_delta),
          significant = wilcox_result$p.value < 0.05,
          n_celltype = length(group1_data),
          n_epcam = length(group2_data)
        )
        
        # Print results immediately
        cat(sprintf("  p-value: %.4f (%s)\n", wilcox_result$p.value, 
                    ifelse(wilcox_result$p.value < 0.05, "significant", "not significant")))
        cat(sprintf("  Medians: %.3f vs %.3f (diff: %.3f)\n", median1, median2, median1 - median2))
        cat(sprintf("  Effect size: %.3f (%s)\n", cliff_delta, interpret_cliff_delta(cliff_delta)))
        cat(sprintf("  Sample sizes: %d vs %d\n", length(group1_data), length(group2_data)))
      } else {
        cat(sprintf("  Skipped - insufficient data after removing NAs\n"))
      }
    } else {
      cat(sprintf("  Skipped - missing one or both groups\n"))
    }
  }
  
  return(test_results)
}

# Function to apply multiple testing correction
apply_multiple_testing_correction <- function(test_results, method = "BH") {
  if (length(test_results) == 0) return(test_results)
  
  # Extract p-values
  p_values <- sapply(test_results, function(x) x$p_value)
  
  # Apply correction
  adjusted_p <- p.adjust(p_values, method = method)
  
  # Update results with adjusted p-values
  for (i in seq_along(test_results)) {
    test_results[[i]]$p_value_adjusted <- adjusted_p[i]
    test_results[[i]]$significant_adjusted <- adjusted_p[i] < 0.05
  }
  
  return(test_results)
}

# Create output directory if it doesn't exist
if (!dir.exists("250725.Fig7Final")) {
  dir.create("250725.Fig7Final", recursive = TRUE)
  cat("Created directory: 250725.Fig7Final\n")
}

# Print the gene list to verify
cat("Gene list to analyze:\n")
for (i in seq_along(genes_of_interest)) {
  cat(sprintf("%d. %s\n", i, genes_of_interest[i]))
}

# Check which genes exist in the dataset
cat("\nChecking gene availability in dataset...\n")
if (exists("merged_data_all_7")) {
  available_genes <- genes_of_interest[genes_of_interest %in% rownames(merged_data_all_7)]
  missing_genes <- genes_of_interest[!genes_of_interest %in% rownames(merged_data_all_7)]
  
  cat(sprintf("Available genes (%d): %s\n", length(available_genes), paste(available_genes, collapse = ", ")))
  if (length(missing_genes) > 0) {
    cat(sprintf("Missing genes (%d): %s\n", length(missing_genes), paste(missing_genes, collapse = ", ")))
    
    # Try to find similar gene names for missing genes
    for (missing_gene in missing_genes) {
      cat(sprintf("\nSearching for genes similar to '%s':\n", missing_gene))
      # Look for genes that contain the missing gene name (case-insensitive)
      similar_genes <- grep(missing_gene, rownames(merged_data_all_7), value = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE)
      if (length(similar_genes) > 0) {
        cat(sprintf("  Found: %s\n", paste(head(similar_genes, 5), collapse = ", ")))
      } else {
        cat("  No similar genes found\n")
      }
    }
  }
} else {
  cat("ERROR: merged_data_all_7 object not found!\n")
  stop("Please ensure the Seurat object 'merged_data_all_7' is loaded in your environment.")
}

# Test with first available gene to make sure everything works
if (length(available_genes) > 0) {
  test_gene <- available_genes[1]
  cat(sprintf("\nTesting with first available gene: %s\n", test_gene))
  
  # Test the analysis function
  test_results <- perform_wilcoxon_tests(merged_data_all_7, test_gene)
  
  if (length(test_results) > 0) {
    cat("\nAnalysis function working! Proceeding with all available genes...\n")
    
    # Now run the full analysis
    all_results <- list()
    
    # Create plots for each gene (only available ones)
    vln_plots_refined <- list()
    
    for (i in seq_along(available_genes)) {
      gene <- available_genes[i]
      cat(sprintf("\n### Processing gene %d/%d: %s ###\n", i, length(available_genes), gene))
      
      # Perform statistical tests
      stats_results <- perform_wilcoxon_tests(merged_data_all_7, gene)
      
      # Apply multiple testing correction
      if (length(stats_results) > 0) {
        stats_results <- apply_multiple_testing_correction(stats_results, method = "BH")
        all_results[[gene]] <- stats_results
        
        cat("\nAfter multiple testing correction:\n")
        for (comparison_name in names(stats_results)) {
          result <- stats_results[[comparison_name]]
          cat(sprintf("%s: raw p=%.4f, adj p=%.4f (%s)\n", 
                      result$comparison, result$p_value, result$p_value_adjusted,
                      ifelse(result$significant_adjusted, "significant", "not significant")))
        }
      }
      
      # Create violin plot WITHOUT legend (we'll add a shared legend later)
      cat("Creating violin plot...\n")
      
      tryCatch({
        # First create a basic plot without annotations, excluding CHC
        # Subset the data to exclude CHC
        subset_obj <- subset(merged_data_all_7, subset = RefinedCellType != "CHC")
        
        p <- VlnPlot(
          subset_obj,
          features = gene,
          group.by = "RefinedCellType",
          pt.size = 0.1,
          slot = "data",
          cols = custom_colors_refined
        ) +
          ggtitle(gene) +
          theme_minimal(base_size = 6) +  # Set base text size to 6
          theme(
            axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 6),
            axis.text.y = element_text(size = 6),
            axis.title.x = element_text(size = 6),
            axis.title.y = element_text(size = 6),
            plot.title = element_text(size = 6),
            legend.position = "none",  # Remove individual legends
            plot.margin = margin(5, 5, 5, 5)
          )
        
        cat("Basic violin plot created successfully (CHC excluded, no legend).\n")
        
        # Add significance annotations if we have results
        if (length(stats_results) > 0) {
          cat("Adding significance annotations...\n")
          
          # Get the plot build to access the exact x-axis positions
          plot_build <- ggplot_build(p)
          
          # Extract x-axis labels and their positions from the built plot
          x_labels <- plot_build$layout$panel_params[[1]]$x$get_labels()
          x_positions <- 1:length(x_labels)
          
          cat(sprintf("Plot x-axis order: %s\n", paste(x_labels, collapse = ", ")))
          cat(sprintf("Positions: %s\n", paste(x_positions, collapse = ", ")))
          
          # Get expression data for y-positioning
          plot_data <- FetchData(subset_obj, vars = c(gene, "RefinedCellType"))
          max_expression <- max(plot_data[[1]], na.rm = TRUE)
          min_expression <- min(plot_data[[1]], na.rm = TRUE)
          y_annotation <- min_expression + (max_expression - min_expression) * 0.5  # Middle of the plot
          
          cat(sprintf("Expression range: %.3f to %.3f, annotation y-position: %.3f\n", 
                      min_expression, max_expression, y_annotation))
          
          # Create annotations based on the actual plot order
          sig_annotations <- data.frame()
          
          for (comparison_name in names(stats_results)) {
            result <- stats_results[[comparison_name]]
            cell_type <- strsplit(comparison_name, " vs ")[[1]][1]
            
            # Find the exact position in the plot
            if (cell_type %in% x_labels) {
              cell_position <- which(x_labels == cell_type)
              
              # Determine significance
              if (result$p_value_adjusted < 0.001) {
                sig_symbol <- "***"
              } else if (result$p_value_adjusted < 0.01) {
                sig_symbol <- "**"
              } else if (result$p_value_adjusted < 0.05) {
                sig_symbol <- "*"
              } else {
                sig_symbol <- NULL  # Don't annotate non-significant
              }
              
              # Add to annotations if significant
              if (!is.null(sig_symbol)) {
                sig_annotations <- rbind(sig_annotations, data.frame(
                  x = cell_position,
                  y = y_annotation,
                  label = sig_symbol,
                  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
                ))
                
                cat(sprintf("  %s: %s (plot position %d)\n", cell_type, sig_symbol, cell_position))
              }
            } else {
              cat(sprintf("  %s: not found in plot x-axis\n", cell_type))
            }
          }
          
          # Add annotations to plot if any exist
          if (nrow(sig_annotations) > 0) {
            p <- p +
              # First add black outline (slightly larger)
              annotate("text", 
                       x = sig_annotations$x, 
                       y = sig_annotations$y, 
                       label = sig_annotations$label,
                       size = 4.5,  # Slightly larger for outline effect
                       fontface = "bold",
                       color = "white") +
              # Then add white text on top
              annotate("text", 
                       x = sig_annotations$x, 
                       y = sig_annotations$y, 
                       label = sig_annotations$label,
                       size = 4,  # One size bigger than before (was 3)
                       fontface = "bold",
                       color = "black")
            
            cat(sprintf("Added %d significance annotations in middle of plot\n", nrow(sig_annotations)))
          } else {
            cat("No significant results to annotate\n")
          }
        }
        
        cat("Final plot prepared successfully.\n")
        
        vln_plots_refined[[i]] <- p
        
        # Save individual plot
        ggsave(filename = sprintf("250725.Fig7Final/violin_plot_%s.png", gene),
               plot = p, width = 4, height = 4, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
        ggsave(filename = sprintf("250725.Fig7Final/violin_plot_%s.pdf", gene),
               plot = p, width = 4, height = 4, bg = "white")
        
        cat("Violin plot created and saved successfully!\n")
        
      }, error = function(e) {
        cat(sprintf("Error creating plot for %s: %s\n", gene, e$message))
        vln_plots_refined[[i]] <- NULL  # Set to NULL if plot creation fails
      })
    }
    
    # Create combined plot with shared legend if we have individual plots
    if (length(vln_plots_refined) > 0) {
      cat("\nCreating combined plot with shared legend...\n")
      
      # Filter out any NULL plots
      valid_plots <- vln_plots_refined[!sapply(vln_plots_refined, is.null)]
      
      if (length(valid_plots) > 0) {
        tryCatch({
          # Create a dummy plot just to extract the legend
          subset_obj <- subset(merged_data_all_7, subset = RefinedCellType != "CHC")
          
          dummy_plot <- VlnPlot(
            subset_obj,
            features = available_genes[1],  # Use first available gene
            group.by = "RefinedCellType",
            pt.size = 0.1,
            slot = "data",
            cols = custom_colors_refined
          ) +
            theme_minimal() +
            theme(
              legend.text = element_text(size = 8),
              legend.title = element_text(size = 8)
            )
          
          # Extract the legend
          legend <- cowplot::get_legend(dummy_plot)
          
          # Combine plots without legends
          combined_plot_no_legend <- wrap_plots(valid_plots, ncol = 3)
          
          # Add the shared legend to the right
          combined_plot <- cowplot::plot_grid(
            combined_plot_no_legend, 
            legend, 
            ncol = 2, 
            rel_widths = c(1, 0.1)
          )
          
          # Save combined plot
          ggsave(filename = "250725.Fig7Final/violin_plots_combined.png",
                 plot = combined_plot, width = 13, height = 8, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
          ggsave(filename = "250725.Fig7Final/violin_plots_combined.pdf",
                 plot = combined_plot, width = 13, height = 8, bg = "white")
          
          cat(sprintf("Combined plot with shared legend saved successfully with %d plots!\n", length(valid_plots)))
          
          # Display the combined plot
          print(combined_plot)
          
        }, error = function(e) {
          cat(sprintf("Error creating combined plot: %s\n", e$message))
          cat("Trying alternative approach without cowplot...\n")
          
          # Alternative approach using patchwork with guide collection
          tryCatch({
            combined_plot <- wrap_plots(valid_plots, ncol = 3) + 
              plot_layout(guides = "collect") & 
              theme(legend.position = "right")
            
            # Save combined plot
            ggsave(filename = "250725.Fig7Final/violin_plots_combined.png",
                   plot = combined_plot, width = 13, height = 8, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
            ggsave(filename = "250725.Fig7Final/violin_plots_combined.pdf",
                   plot = combined_plot, width = 13, height = 8, bg = "white")
            
            cat(sprintf("Combined plot (alternative method) saved successfully with %d plots!\n", length(valid_plots)))
            print(combined_plot)
            
          }, error = function(e2) {
            cat(sprintf("Alternative approach also failed: %s\n", e2$message))
          })
        })
      } else {
        cat("No valid plots to combine.\n")
      }
    }
    
    cat("\n=== ANALYSIS COMPLETE ===\n")
    cat("Files saved to 250725.Fig7Final/\n")
    cat("- Individual plots: violin_plot_[GENE].png/pdf\n")
    cat("- Combined plot: violin_plots_combined.png/pdf\n")
    
    # Summary of missing genes
    if (length(missing_genes) > 0) {
      cat(sprintf("\nNOTE: %d gene(s) were missing from the dataset: %s\n", 
                  length(missing_genes), paste(missing_genes, collapse = ", ")))
      cat("Please check if these genes have different names in your dataset.\n")
    }
    
  } else {
    cat("No results from test analysis. Please check your data and gene names.\n")
  }
} else {
  cat("ERROR: No genes from the list were found in the dataset!\n")
  cat("Please check your gene names and dataset.\n")
}
```
